description:
A technical writer wants to gather a **one–page, plain-text snapshot** of basic
    system health so they can paste it into user documentation.  
    Your job is to create that snapshot on the command line and save it to disk.

    1. Create the directory **/home/user/monitoring** (if it is not already
       present).
    2. Inside that directory, create the file  
       **/home/user/monitoring/sys_report.log**.  
       • If a file by that name already exists, overwrite it completely.  
       • The file must be pure UTF-8 text.
    3. Append the live output of exactly three diagnostic commands, in this
       order, to the file:  
       a. `uptime`  
       b. `free -h`  
       c. `df -h /` (note the single forward-slash argument)  
       You may capture the command output with redirection (`>>`), `tee`, or any
       other shell technique, but **do not** copy-type the results manually.
    4. The file **must follow this exact layout** (blank lines are significant;
       the automated test will look for them literally).  Angle-bracketed text
       represents dynamic content you must fill in; everything else is
       verbatim:

       ----- System Diagnostics Log -----
       Timestamp: <ISO-8601-UTC timestamp, e.g. 2024-03-21T14:37:12Z>

       === OUTPUT: uptime ===
       <actual output of the uptime command>

       === OUTPUT: free -h ===
       <actual output of the free -h command>

       === OUTPUT: df -h / ===
       <actual output of the df -h / command>

       (End of file)

       • The first line (dashes + title) must begin at column 1.  
       • The timestamp must be generated at run time; use UTC in the format
         `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.  
       • Exactly one blank line must exist:
         – after the Timestamp line,  
         – after each “=== OUTPUT … ===” header.
    5. After writing the file, show its complete contents in the terminal so the
       technical writer can visually confirm what was captured.

    Result expected by the reviewer:
    • Directory /home/user/monitoring exists.  
    • File /home/user/monitoring/sys_report.log exists, is non-empty, and
      conforms *precisely* to the layout above (header lines, blank lines, and
      section order).  
    • The three required command outputs are present beneath their respective
      headers.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth verification data used by the automated grader.           -->
    <!-- DO NOT reveal any of this in the public task description.             -->

    directory_required:/home/user/monitoring

    file_required:/home/user/monitoring/sys_report.log

    file_must_contain_exact_line_1:----- System Diagnostics Log -----
    file_must_contain_exact_line_2_prefix:Timestamp: 

    section_headers_in_order:
        1:=== OUTPUT: uptime ===
        2:=== OUTPUT: free -h ===
        3:=== OUTPUT: df -h / ===

    blank_line_rules:
        - A single blank line follows the Timestamp line.
        - A single blank line follows each section header line.

    # The grader will allow any bytes between section headers, so long as at
    # least one non-empty line (the command output) exists under each header.
name: task_000000_a719d6da
